scope.target: Plan the missing Codex runtime skill wrapper layer for portable canonical skills
constraints: Keep repo portability as the primary design goal
Do not collapse prompt wrappers and runtime skill wrappers into one concept
non_goals: Do not implement the Codex runtime skill wrappers yet
Do not install anything into $CODEX_HOME/skills in this planning step

inputs.architecture_target: Define how portable canonical skills should map into Codex runtime skills without duplicating the canonical source
review_findings: Current .codex/prompts wrappers are prompt-oriented and do not satisfy the expectation of Codex-native runtime skills
user_constraints: The solution should stay highly portable and repo-centric
The planning work should be recorded under .dev/workflows

steps: Separated Codex prompt wrappers from Codex runtime skill wrappers as different layers
Defined a pilot plan for ddd-ca-hex-architect
Recorded the follow-up scope inside the existing portable AI asset architecture workflow
validation: The plan distinguishes canonical source, human guide, prompt wrappers, and runtime skill wrappers
The plan keeps .ai/assets as the canonical source of truth
The plan does not require copying full skill content into Codex-specific directories

results.summary: Planned the missing Codex runtime skill wrapper layer as a follow-up to the portable AI asset architecture rollout.
